qa.overall_assessment: BA addressed both prior objections (OBJ-1 wrong channel, OBJ-2 report-schema.json excluded). Dimensions 1-4 now pass. Two new blocking defects in dimension 5: (A) context-20260517-153856.json is invalid JSON (unescaped CJK curly-quote characters at line 7, python3 -m json.tool fails); (B) AC1 line 120 instructs direct $ref to finding_base which would strip finding_deterministic/contextual/aesthetic channel-specific constraints if followed literally. Minor non-blocking: AC6 lacks file-scoped verification recipe for dirty working tree.

OBJ-1.original: AC1 was targeting the wrong channel (prose example issues[] instead of report-schema.json). BA now targets skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json definitions.finding_base.
OBJ-1.verification: AC1 now reads: 'GIVEN skills/ui-shared/report-schema.json definitions.finding_base / WHEN dev reads the finding_base schema / THEN location is a structured object...' — confirmed targeting finding_base in report-schema.json.

OBJ-2.original: report-schema.json was excluded from scope while claiming machine-enforceability. BA now includes it in scope.
OBJ-2.verification: report-schema.json is now listed as file 1 of 6 in scope (ticket:43-44), in affected_files, in files_to_modify, and in AC6 (ticket:155). R0 explicitly covers it as a prerequisite clause.

objections.title: context-20260517-153856.json is invalid JSON — dev cannot parse BA deliverable
objections.claim: BA produced a valid JSON context file consumable by the dev workflow
objections.problem: python3 -m json.tool docs/dev/context-20260517-153856.json fails: Expecting ',' delimiter: line 7 column 187. The requirement.original string at line 7 contains CJK curly-quote characters (U+201C U+201D) around the Chinese phrase inside a JSON string. These are not valid JSON and are not escaped. Dev workflow reads this file as structured input — parse failure blocks the cycle.
objections.required_evidence: BA must regenerate context-20260517-153856.json with valid JSON. Verify with: python3 -m json.tool docs/dev/context-20260517-153856.json && echo VALID.

evidence.cause: line 7 requirement.original string contains unescaped CJK curly quotes around the Chinese phrase

objections.title: AC1 line 120: direct $ref to finding_base would strip channel-specific schema constraints
objections.claim: AC1 ticket:120 says: 'all three canonical channels use $ref: "#/definitions/finding_base" so they inherit these fields'
objections.problem: Current schema routes channel arrays to finding_deterministic, finding_contextual, finding_aesthetic (each uses allOf to include finding_base). These channel-specific definitions enforce: finding_deterministic requires axe_impact, evidence_mode const 'deterministic', computed_value, expected_value; finding_contextual requires requires_human; finding_aesthetic requires category, severity_capped_at, advisory (the double-defense severity hard-cap). If dev follows AC1 literally and changes channel items to directly reference finding_base, all channel-specific required fields and constraints are removed. This is a regression that breaks schema-enforced quality gates. The intent (inherit new base fields) is correct; the mechanism (replace channel $refs) is wrong. The correct mechanism is adding fields to finding_base, which all three channel definitions already include via allOf.
objections.required_evidence: BA must reword AC1 line 120 to: 'AND the three canonical channel definitions (finding_deterministic, finding_contextual, finding_aesthetic) continue to reference finding_base via allOf — DO NOT replace channel $refs with direct $ref to finding_base. New base fields are inherited automatically via the existing allOf pattern.' The Technical Hints (ticket:163) already correctly state the approach — AC1 must be consistent with those hints.

channel_constraints_at_risk.finding_deterministic: lines 235-246: allOf includes axe_impact, computed_value, expected_value, evidence_mode const
channel_constraints_at_risk.finding_aesthetic: lines 261-274: allOf requires category, severity_capped_at, advisory — double-defense hard-cap

evidence.technical_hints_correct: ticket:163: 'change location from type:string to structured object...add measured, expected, downstream_agent to finding_base.properties' — implies finding_base modification, not channel $ref replacement
evidence.ac1_conflicting_line: ticket:120: 'use $ref: "#/definitions/finding_base" so they inherit these fields' — contradicts hints

objections.title: AC6 verification recipe ambiguous in dirty working tree
objections.claim: AC6 ticket:152-157: 'GIVEN git diff for this cycle / WHEN reviewed / THEN exactly these 6 files show changes'
objections.problem: Working tree has 31 pre-existing modified/untracked files (git status --short confirmed). Raw git diff will include all pre-existing changes, making 'exactly 6 files' unverifiable with that recipe. QA would need a file-scoped command to confirm the cycle only touched the 6 named files.
objections.required_evidence: BA should reword AC6 to: 'WHEN dev-introduced changes are verified by checking git diff HEAD -- <each of the 6 named files>, THEN each shows a diff AND git diff HEAD -- commands/dev.md returns empty AND git diff HEAD -- agents/pm.md returns empty'.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) returned 5 findings: (1) BLOCKER context JSON invalid; (2) BLOCKER commands/dev.md dispatch template missing specialist_findings; (3) MAJOR AC1 $ref wording breaks channel schemas; (4) MAJOR nullable location fields non-actionable; (5) MAJOR AC6 dirty tree. QA incorporated findings 1, 3, 5 as OBJ-3, OBJ-4, OBJ-5. Findings 2, 4 classified out_of_scope.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: OBJ-3, OBJ-4, OBJ-5 from codex findings 1, 3, 5. Findings 2 and 4 logged as observations only.
